# UC-18.3.9 · VXLAN VTEP Reachability

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Fault, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the tunnel endpoints that stitch your switches together for app traffic, so broken neighbors show up early and you can fix them before people feel disconnects.*

---

## Description

Tracks **NVE** peer reachability for **VXLAN** fabrics by normalizing **`show nve peers`** style feeds (**peer_ip**, **vtep_ip**, **peer_state** **Up**/**Down**, **vni**, **peer_learn_type**, **uptime**), augmenting with **`nxos:nve_counters`** drop/error hints, **`network:vxlan_tunnel`** **oper** posture, **`cisco:nxos:syslog`** **%NVE-** / tunnel-down text, and **`network:bgp_neighbor`** **EVPN** session health so operators see overlay isolation, partial degradation, or control-plane churn before east-west and north-south tests fail.

## Value

Tenant **L2VNI**/**L3VNI** segments ride on **VTEP** reachability; a single stale or down peer can strand **VM**/**pod** traffic, elongate failover, or black-hole ARP/ND while the underlay still pings. Surfacing **peer_state** slips beside **BGP** and counter corroboration ties bridge calls to the right leaf-spine hop and protects revenue workloads, **SLA** windows, and change freezes from mystery micro-outages.

## Implementation

Schedule **NX-API** (**ins_api**) or **1546** **REST** stanzas for **`show nve peers`** / **`show nve counters`** on **120–300s** cadence per change policy; normalize **FIELDALIAS** maps for **peer_ip**, **vtep_ip**, **peer_state**, **vni**, **peer_learn_type**, **uptime**; forward **NX-OS** syslog via **SC4S**/UF; ingest **EVPN** **BGP** neighbor polls as **network:bgp_neighbor**; save **uc_18_3_9_vtep_core** and clone **uc_18_3_9_vtep_page** with **CMDB** suppression and **risk** thresholds.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Governed indexes (**dc_fabric**, **network_ops**, **network**) per classification; **RBAC** hides management VTEP IPs where required—never paste operational secrets into tickets.
- NX-OS 10.2+ leaves running **NVE1** (or RIB-attached **NVE**) with **VPC** and **EVPN** baselines documented. Read-only **NX-API** must allow show nve peers, show nve counters, optional show tunnel-encryption, and show bgp l2vpn evpn summary.
- Heavy Forwarder path to **NX-API** on HTTPS tcp/443 with pinned trust; use outbound TCP/443 only from the collection tier—do not tunnel operator passwords through ad hoc REST bodies or SMB-hosted scripts.
- Automation credentials live in **passwords.conf** for **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)); stagger polls with jitter so hundreds of leaves do not hit HTTP 429 simultaneously.
- **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) layouts are templates—rebind macros so panels read your real indexes and sourcetype strings.
- Optional **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** ([Splunkbase 1897](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1897)) when APIC MO health must corroborate NVE tables during brownfield moves.
- Optional **Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX** ([Splunkbase 4241](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4241)) and **Splunk Add-on for VMware** ([Splunkbase 3215](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3215)) help time vMotion waves against VTEP churn— they are not substitutes for switch-native peer tables.
- Maintain fabric_peer_inventory.csv with expected **peer_ip** counts per leaf/VNI so nightly diffs catch silent peer loss versus telemetry gaps.
- One lab leaf to snapshot show nve peers versus Splunk JSON rows before production cutover.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
- **nxos:nve_peers** — NX-API ins_api executing show nve peers every 120–300s (60s only during ISSU windows approved by TAC). Emit peer_ip, vtep_ip, peer_state (Up/Down), vni, peer_learn_type, uptime. Expect kilobytes of JSON per poll when peer tables are large—provision index volume and retention accordingly.
- **nxos:nve_counters** — show nve counters or NVE-scoped interface counters every five to fifteen minutes; map discard and error banks into numeric fields for ctr_fault evaluation. Tag collector_id when multiple automations hit the same switch.
- **network:vxlan_tunnel** — modeled tunnel oper state from streaming telemetry, gRPC, or NMS exports; tunnel_state and oper_state must mirror CLI semantics operators trust.
- **cisco:nxos:syslog** — %NVE- and tunnel-down narratives with TIME_FORMAT aligned to NTP on both leaf and Splunk indexer tiers.
- **network:bgp_neighbor** — EVPN neighbor polling with bgp_state and neighbor address so control-plane loss accompanies NVE symptoms in the same search window.
- Post-onboarding QA: run tstats count over nxos:nve_peers and nxos:nve_counters by host to prove parity across pods before alerting goes live.

Field contracts for parsers: **peer_ip** (remote **VTEP**), **vtep_ip** (local **RLOC** on the leaf), **peer_state** normalized to **Up**/**Down**/**Active** synonyms, decimal **vni** strings, **peer_learn_type** carrying **control-plane** versus **data-plane** semantics, and **uptime** copied faithfully from **CLI** timers. The **host** field must remain the originating **NX-OS** device even when **JSON** wraps **NX-API** output.

**Sourcetype** fidelity checklist: **nxos:nve_peers** never mixes with **nxos:nve_counters** in the same **LINE_BREAKER**; **cisco:nxos:syslog** keeps **%** mnemonics intact; **network:bgp_neighbor** records **AFI**/**SAFI** for **EVPN**; **network:vxlan_tunnel** exposes **tunnel_state** separate from **admin** intent.

**props.conf** hygiene: **SHOULD_LINEMERGE** false for **JSON** **NX-API** blobs; **TIMESTAMP_FIELDS** explicit on **syslog**; **TRANSFORMS** only after validating **sample** events in lab.



### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save uc_18_3_9_vtep_core (investigation) and uc_18_3_9_vtep_page (paging). Wrap indexes in macro_dc_fabric_indexes so clones work across dev and prod.

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network) earliest=-4h@m latest=now
(
  sourcetype="nxos:nve_peers" OR sourcetype="nxos:nve_counters" OR
  sourcetype="network:vxlan_tunnel" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR
  sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor"
)
| eval leaf=trim(lower(coalesce(host,hostname,device_name,switch_id,dvc,"")))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peer_ip,peer,nve_peer_ip,remote_vtep,bgp_neighbor,neighbor,evpn_peer,""))
| eval vtep_ip=trim(coalesce(vtep_ip,local_vtep,nve_source_ip,src_vtep,source_addr,""))
| eval peer_state=upper(trim(coalesce(peer_state,nve_peer_state,oper_state,peer_oper_state,nve_state,"")))
| eval vni=trim(coalesce(vni,l2vni,l3vni,evpn_vni,vni_list,""))
| eval peer_learn_type=lower(trim(coalesce(peer_learn_type,learn_type,learning_mode,learn_mode,mode,"")))
| eval uptime=trim(coalesce(uptime,peer_uptime,up_duration,peer_up_time,state_time,""))
| eval st=sourcetype
| eval nve_row=if(st="nxos:nve_peers",1,0)
| eval up_ok=if(nve_row=1,
    case(
      match(peer_state,"^(UP|ACTIVE)$"),1,
      match(peer_state,"UP|REACHABLE"),1,
      true(),0
    ),null())
| eval syslog_fault=if(st="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(%NVE-|NVE\\s*peer|nve.*(down|delete)|vxlan.*tunnel.*(down|fail))"),1,0)
| eval tun_fault=if(st="network:vxlan_tunnel" AND match(lower(trim(coalesce(tunnel_oper,oper_state,tunnel_state,status,""))),"down|fail|unknown|invalid|deleted"),1,0)
| eval ctr_fault=if(st="nxos:nve_counters" AND (
      tonumber(coalesce(rx_drop,tx_drop,in_discards,out_discards,dropped_pkts,0))>0 OR
      tonumber(coalesce(error_pkts,integrity_err,hdr_err,0))>0
    ),1,0)
| eval bgp_fault=if(st="network:bgp_neighbor" AND NOT match(lower(trim(coalesce(bgp_state,state,fsm,""))),"estab"),1,0)
| where nve_row=1 OR syslog_fault=1 OR tun_fault=1 OR ctr_fault=1 OR bgp_fault=1
| bin _time span=5m
| stats latest(vtep_ip) AS vtep_ip latest(vni) AS vni latest(peer_learn_type) AS peer_learn_type latest(uptime) AS uptime
    max(up_ok) AS up_clip min(up_ok) AS low_clip
    max(syslog_fault) AS fl_sys max(tun_fault) AS fl_tun max(ctr_fault) AS fl_ctr max(bgp_fault) AS fl_bgp
    values(st) AS sourcetype_mix count AS samples
    BY _time leaf peer_ip
| eval peer_state_out=case(
    fl_tun>0 OR fl_sys>0 OR low_clip=0,"Down",
    fl_ctr>0 OR fl_bgp>0,"Degraded",
    true(),"Up")
| eval risk=case(peer_state_out="Down",100, peer_state_out="Degraded",55, true(),0) + fl_sys*12 + fl_tun*10 + fl_bgp*8 + fl_ctr*5
| where peer_state_out!="Up" AND len(peer_ip)>0
| sort - risk - _time
| head 250
| table _time leaf peer_ip vtep_ip vni peer_learn_type peer_state_out uptime risk fl_sys fl_tun fl_ctr fl_bgp sourcetype_mix samples
```

#### Understanding this SPL
**Search performance** — Keep leading **sourcetype** clauses inside the first pipeline predicate; widen **earliest** only when investigating past **post-mortems**; on **petabyte-scale** **tiers**, mirror a **subset** of **nxos:nve_peers** into a warm **summary** index for **executive** tiles.


**Scope** — Unifies authoritative **nxos:nve_peers** peer_state readings with supporting **nxos:nve_counters**, **network:vxlan_tunnel**, **cisco:nxos:syslog**, and **network:bgp_neighbor** signals so Down is rarely a single-feed false trip.

**Normalization** — coalesce() merges field renames across NX-OS maintenance; leaf collapses hostname aliases for consistent drilldowns.

**State math** — up_ok gates on nxos:nve_peers rows only; stats min(up_ok) inside five-minute bins catches brief Down intervals. fl_sys and fl_tun elevate inconsistencies where syslog or tunnel models disagree with JSON that still lists Up.

**Risk weights** — Down baseline 100 plus syslog, tunnel, BGP, and counter multipliers—raise fl_bgp if route-reflector soft-reconfig noise dominates, or emphasize fl_sys when %NVE lines are your fastest incident truth.

**Filtering** — Terminal where removes rows lacking peer_ip to suppress malformed syslog; add rex extractions if %NVE text omits structured peer fields.

**Pipeline** — Per-sourcetype booleans, time-bucketed stats by leaf and peer_ip, peer_state_out classification, risk ranking, and a NOC-facing table.

### Step 3 — Validate
- CLI show nve peers Peer-IP and State tuples versus nxos:nve_peers for two known VNIs; tolerate one poll interval skew.
- show nve counters discard totals versus ctr_fault spikes; watch for 32-bit rollover stickiness on long-lived platforms.
- show bgp l2vpn evpn summary versus network:bgp_neighbor on the same RR/leaf relationship.
- timechart span=15m count by sourcetype; flat nxos:nve_peers volume after a credential rotation often correlates to HTTP 403 or TLS trust errors in splunkd.log for the REST modular input stanza.
- network:vxlan_tunnel down samples versus show nve interface nve1 ifOperStatus on the reporting leaf.
- Map Cisco NX-OS documentation language for NVE peers and learn types to your EVPN design so Splunk field semantics stay aligned with how operators were trained.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
- Tier-1 wall: single-value Down peer counts per site linked to a peer matrix dashboard.
- Tier-2 analyst view: timechart of risk by pod with change-request overlays from ITSM.
- Tier-3 forensic row: raw syslog drilldown filtered on leaf and peer_ip without exporting secrets.
- Runbook tie-in: confirm underlay MTU, BUM handling, and VPC consistency before entertaining NVE hardware replacements.
- Schedule the saved search every five minutes; optional summary indexing for rolling peer stability SLAs.
- **KPI** hygiene: track **mean_time_to_detect** for **VTEP** incidents alongside **risk** so leadership sees **overlay** health, not only device uptime.


### Step 5 — Visualization, alerting, and troubleshooting
**Visualization** — Build Dashboard Studio or Simple XML views: peer matrix of leaf by peer_ip colored with peer_state_out; companion timechart for risk; detail table listing vni, peer_learn_type, uptime, and fault flags; optional BGP state facet per pod.

**Alert design** — uc_18_3_9_vtep_page should fire when risk crosses 110 or peer_state_out stays Down for two consecutive five-minute buckets for the same leaf and peer_ip combination; throttle 30–45 minutes; honor maintenance_inventory.csv; include vtep_ip, vni, fl_* booleans, and sourcetype_mix in payloads—never operator credentials.

**Troubleshooting** — If nxos:nve_peers stops entirely, verify feature nxapi, correct VDC, and a manual show nve peers on device. Null peer_ip after upgrades almost always signals FIELDALIAS or JSON path drift—patch props and transforms. BGP faults without NVE down point to RR partitioning or import policy—treat as underlay or control-plane queues before blaming virtualized workloads. Transient ctr_fault after clear counters should be ignored unless nonzero across multiple intervals or backed by a maintenance record. Duplicate FQDN versus short-name hosts split aggregates—enforce CMDB canonical leaf before tuning thresholds. Elevated samples with continuously Up peers suggests poll intervals that are too coarse for the incident pattern you need to catch—tighten collection before raising alert sensitivity.

Scripted alternative (optional)
When REST is unacceptable, scripted inputs can SSH show nve peers every three minutes printing key=value lines—use docs/implementation-guide.md for paths, service accounts, and checkpoint files.


## SPL

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network) earliest=-4h@m latest=now
(
  sourcetype="nxos:nve_peers" OR sourcetype="nxos:nve_counters" OR
  sourcetype="network:vxlan_tunnel" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR
  sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor"
)
| eval leaf=trim(lower(coalesce(host,hostname,device_name,switch_id,dvc,"")))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peer_ip,peer,nve_peer_ip,remote_vtep,bgp_neighbor,neighbor,evpn_peer,""))
| eval vtep_ip=trim(coalesce(vtep_ip,local_vtep,nve_source_ip,src_vtep,source_addr,""))
| eval peer_state=upper(trim(coalesce(peer_state,nve_peer_state,oper_state,peer_oper_state,nve_state,"")))
| eval vni=trim(coalesce(vni,l2vni,l3vni,evpn_vni,vni_list,""))
| eval peer_learn_type=lower(trim(coalesce(peer_learn_type,learn_type,learning_mode,learn_mode,mode,"")))
| eval uptime=trim(coalesce(uptime,peer_uptime,up_duration,peer_up_time,state_time,""))
| eval st=sourcetype
| eval nve_row=if(st="nxos:nve_peers",1,0)
| eval up_ok=if(nve_row=1,
    case(
      match(peer_state,"^(UP|ACTIVE)$"),1,
      match(peer_state,"UP|REACHABLE"),1,
      true(),0
    ),null())
| eval syslog_fault=if(st="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(%NVE-|NVE\\s*peer|nve.*(down|delete)|vxlan.*tunnel.*(down|fail))"),1,0)
| eval tun_fault=if(st="network:vxlan_tunnel" AND match(lower(trim(coalesce(tunnel_oper,oper_state,tunnel_state,status,""))),"down|fail|unknown|invalid|deleted"),1,0)
| eval ctr_fault=if(st="nxos:nve_counters" AND (
      tonumber(coalesce(rx_drop,tx_drop,in_discards,out_discards,dropped_pkts,0))>0 OR
      tonumber(coalesce(error_pkts,integrity_err,hdr_err,0))>0
    ),1,0)
| eval bgp_fault=if(st="network:bgp_neighbor" AND NOT match(lower(trim(coalesce(bgp_state,state,fsm,""))),"estab"),1,0)
| where nve_row=1 OR syslog_fault=1 OR tun_fault=1 OR ctr_fault=1 OR bgp_fault=1
| bin _time span=5m
| stats latest(vtep_ip) AS vtep_ip latest(vni) AS vni latest(peer_learn_type) AS peer_learn_type latest(uptime) AS uptime
    max(up_ok) AS up_clip min(up_ok) AS low_clip
    max(syslog_fault) AS fl_sys max(tun_fault) AS fl_tun max(ctr_fault) AS fl_ctr max(bgp_fault) AS fl_bgp
    values(st) AS sourcetype_mix count AS samples
    BY _time leaf peer_ip
| eval peer_state_out=case(
    fl_tun>0 OR fl_sys>0 OR low_clip=0,"Down",
    fl_ctr>0 OR fl_bgp>0,"Degraded",
    true(),"Up")
| eval risk=case(peer_state_out="Down",100, peer_state_out="Degraded",55, true(),0) + fl_sys*12 + fl_tun*10 + fl_bgp*8 + fl_ctr*5
| where peer_state_out!="Up" AND len(peer_ip)>0
| sort - risk - _time
| head 250
| table _time leaf peer_ip vtep_ip vni peer_learn_type peer_state_out uptime risk fl_sys fl_tun fl_ctr fl_bgp sourcetype_mix samples
```

## Visualization

(1) **Status matrix** — **leaf** × **peer_ip** with **peer_state** color (**Up**/**Down**/ **Degraded**). (2) **Single value** — count of **Down** **VTEP** peers last hour. (3) **Timechart** — **risk** max/min by **pod**. (4) **Table** — **vni**, **peer_learn_type**, **uptime**, **fl_sys**/**fl_bgp** flags for **ITSM**. (5) **Histogram** — **samples** distribution to catch sparse polling.

## Known False Positives

**planned_maintenance_holddown** — Maintenance windows that admin-shut **NVE** interfaces or **EVPN** neighbors under **change** control will mark **peer_state** **Down** in **`nxos:nve_peers`** while **CMDB** still shows production. Honor **suppress_until** lists and **CHG** tags before paging overlay owners.

**issu_fast_reload_blip** — **ISSU**/supervisor switchovers may omit one **NX-API** **poll**, producing **`low_clip=0`** in a single **5m** bucket even though **`show nve peers`** stabilizes seconds later. Require **consecutive** buckets or **`fl_sys`** corroboration before **Sev-1** bridges.

**bgp_soft_reconfig_noise** — Mass **`soft-reconfiguration`** **inbound** on route-reflector clusters can leave **BGP** **Idle**/**Active** for a few **cycles** without **dataplane** loss. Pair **`bgp_fault`** with **`peer_state_out`** **Down** or **`fl_tun`** before declaring **VXLAN** **blackholes**.

**telemetry_alias_drift** — **TA** upgrades occasionally rename **`peer_oper_state`** fields; **`up_ok`** becomes **null** and **`low_clip`** misfires. Run **field** summaries after every Splunk **deploy** wave and keep **`FIELDALIAS`** for legacy **JSON** keys.

**counter_clear_events** — Operators **`clear counters interface nve1`** reset **drop** banks; **`ctr_fault`** spikes once while **peers** stay **Up**. Ignore **one** **interval** **`fl_ctr`** when **change** notes mention **counter** clears.

**vpc_role_asymmetry** — **vPC** peers can show **transient** **data-plane** learn types during **role** negotiation; **`peer_learn_type`** may flap without **reachability** loss. Compare **`uptime`** trends and **underlay** **ping** meshes before **RMA** **hardware**.

**acronym_collision_syslog** — Non-**NVE** messages containing **VXLAN** marketing strings may match **regex** filters. Narrow **`syslog_fault`** to **`%NVE`** **facility** patterns or **exclude** test **VRF** **tags**.

**stale_tunnel_model** — Some **NMS** **tunnels** lag **CLI** by several **minutes**; **`fl_tun`** alone should not outrank **`nxos:nve_peers`** **Up**. Weight **tunnel** sources lower unless **both** agree.
